content.claim: Government and civil-society actors dispute basic statistics on education finance, international and national student performance test results, and indicators of church-sector participation, undermining the existence of a shared, trusted measurement baseline required for independent feedback loops to evaluate educational reforms.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Contested status itself is reported by civil society rather than quantified as a systematic meta-audit of disagreement magnitude
Does not specify which specific statistics are disputed or by how much
Root cause of disagreement (measurement methodology versus political framing) not isolated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
